title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: In-depth media reporting by media scholar Axel Bruns on Giglietto's presentation at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference in Cape Town. The coverage reviews Giglietto's study on how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and Facebook's changing algorithmic governance model dictate the reach of news URLs.
description: An academic blog post by Axel Bruns covering Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the ICA 2026 conference regarding Facebook's algorithmic impact on news URL engagement.

title: M/C Journal 'twitter' Issue Now Out! | Snurblog - Axel Bruns
snippet: Media announcement listing Giglietto as a featured contributor to a special issue of M/C Journal reflecting on Twitter's 20th anniversary, its legacy, and the research hurdles introduced by API limitations under Elon Musk's ownership.
description: An announcement on Axel Bruns' Snurblog for a special 'twitter' issue of M/C Journal, listing Fabio Giglietto as a featured contributor discussing Twitter's legacy and API research hurdles.

title: Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?
snippet: Event announcement for the 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society, highlighting Giglietto as an expert speaker on a roundtable panel evaluating academic access to platform data and public-interest research in the post-CrowdTangle era.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable panelist discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research at the International Conference on Social Media & Society.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage of Giglietto's research findings regarding Meta's algorithmic curation. The article highlights that Giglietto's work ('A Pretty Blunt Approach') was highlighted on the investigative TV program Report on Rai 3, prompting a formal EU parliamentary inquiry and leading the Italian communications authority (AGCOM) to call him for a hearing on algorithmic transparency.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This news article highlights the societal and political consequences of the Facebook feed algorithms investigated by Fabio Giglietto. It outlines how mainstream political accounts suffered visibility penalties under Meta's content-reduction policies, while extremist pages grew to become the primary sources of information.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino Carlo Bo. The research analyzes over 2.5 million Facebook posts to show how Meta's algorithms significantly reduced visibility for traditional political figures while boosting extremist content.
description: A news article reporting on a University of Urbino study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which analyzes Facebook's algorithm changes and their impact on political and extremist content visibility.
